Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions For Discovery Abuse
Off Calendar Aug-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Geoffrey Hutchinson, Esq. of Branson Brinkop Griffith & Campo, Tel. 650.365.7710, for defendants and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT ANWAR SHAIKH and BENAZIR SHAIKH's Motion For Sanctions For Discovery Abuse. Defendants Anwar Shaikh dba Acer Hotel and Benzair Shaikh's motion is granted. No opposition was filed and Plaintiff failed to engage or respond to Defendants' attempts to meet and confer on the issue. It also appears that Plaintiff has failed to follow previous court orders to comply with discovery obligations and has forced Defendant to return to court and file discovery motions. Failing to respond to discovery and to meet and confer constitutes abuse of the discovery process and subjects Plaintiff to sanctions. Defendants' requests for admissions are deemed admitted by Plaintiffs and Plaintiffs are ordered to respond to all outstanding discovery no later than August 15, 2014. Plaintiffs are further sanctioned in the amount of $1,000, payable to Defendants no later than September 1, 2014. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopts the tentative decision. Defendants ANWAR SHAIKH and BENAZIR SHAIKH's Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Responses to Form Interrogatories is GRANTED, for the reasons set forth in moving papers and motion is unopposed. Plaintiffs, TERRY WHITE and JOSEPH FERREIRA and their counsel Frank Z. Leidman, Esq. are ordered to provide complete, verified responses, without objection, to the Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Demand for Production of Documents, Set One which were served by counsel for Defendants ANWAR SHAIKH dba "ACER HOTEL" and BENAZIR SHAIKH on February 19, 2013 and to which responses were to be served by TERRY WHITE and JOSEPH FERREIRA on or before March 26, 2013. The complete, verified responses, without objection are due within 15 calendar days of the date of service of the Notice of Entry of Order on this motion. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (No appearance by prevailing party; however, proposed order was submitted via email by the prevailing party.) Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Peter J. Van Zandt; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
